tasks.description: Create a single exported metadata artifact derived from packages/kumo/scripts/theme-generator/config.ts.
steps: Verify current branch is `geoquant/color-docs`
`@cloudflare/kumo` publishes `@cloudflare/kumo/ai/theme-metadata.json`
Metadata includes available themes and semantic token records for text, color, and typography
Metadata shape is covered by tests or validation checks
Existing public style export paths and CSS variable names remain unchanged

tasks.description: Refactor packages/kumo-docs-astro/src/pages/colors.astro so theme/token sections are driven by exported metadata instead of hardcoded lists that drift.
steps: Verify current branch is `geoquant/color-docs`
Colors page no longer hardcodes available theme names
Colors page no longer claims global tokens unless metadata includes them
Colors page no longer claims a header theme toggle if only mode toggle exists
Adding or removing a theme in source metadata updates docs output without hand-editing theme lists
The current colors docs UI/layout remains materially the same

tasks.description: Refactor packages/kumo-docs-astro/src/components/demos/ColorsDemo.tsx to consume exported metadata and demonstrate scoped data-theme behavior correctly.
steps: Verify current branch is `geoquant/color-docs`
Demo consumes exported metadata rather than the old docs-only ad-hoc token model
Demo removes dead global token logic unless real globals exist
Demo proves overrides work when `data-theme` is applied on a local preview container, not only `body`
Docs copy and demo behavior match each other

tasks.description: Move semantic token discovery in registry/Figma-adjacent tooling to exported metadata where feasible.
steps: Verify current branch is `geoquant/color-docs`
packages/kumo/scripts/component-registry/utils.ts no longer requires `light-dark(` regex scraping to discover semantic colors
packages/kumo-figma semantic color/token metadata consumers read exported metadata instead of sibling source config/CSS where scoped by this PRD
Any intentionally retained non-color CSS parsing is documented in code or spec notes
Figma drift-detection coverage still passes

tasks.description: Verify all remaining `light-dark()` dependencies, then remove or constrain them only where safe without breaking downstream consumers.
steps: Verify current branch is `geoquant/color-docs`
Remaining direct `light-dark()` usages are enumerated before cleanup
If generator output stops emitting `light-dark()`, semantic tokens still resolve correctly via explicit `[data-mode]` scoped vars
If any `light-dark()` usage must remain, docs and code comments reflect the real reason
No downstream consumer breakage in docs, kumo package exports, or figma token generation
Semantic token names and public CSS/style entrypoints remain unchanged for downstream consumers

tasks.description: Validate the end-to-end result across kumo, docs, and figma.
steps: Verify current branch is `geoquant/color-docs`
`@cloudflare/kumo` theme generator tests pass
`@cloudflare/kumo` package export validation passes
`@cloudflare/kumo-docs-astro` build and typecheck pass
`@cloudflare/kumo-figma` data-generation and drift-detection tests pass

patterns: Export machine-readable artifacts from `@cloudflare/kumo/ai/*` when other packages need shared generated data
Keep public package contracts additive; do not expose package-internal generator modules as the main public API
Prefer explicit `[data-mode]` scoped runtime behavior over docs assumptions about `light-dark()`
nonGoals: Do not create a typed JS/TS module as the primary public theme metadata contract in this effort
Do not fully remove all sibling source reads in `packages/kumo-figma`; limit scope to semantic color/token metadata
Do not change public CSS variable names or style export paths
Do not perform implementation work on any branch other than `geoquant/color-docs`
Do not redesign the colors docs UI
